news.t: Bank of Korea Warns Inflation May Stay Elevated Despite Middle East Truce
Bank of Korea Governor Signals Readiness to Raise Rates as Inflation Risks Mount
South Korea Has a Chip Conundrum—Huge Profits and a Serious Selloff
A massive 16% market swing just rocked South Korea over 24 hours. The retail ‘ants' holding the wheel are driving dangerously.
Here's why South Korea's Kospi Index is rising today (June 9)
Korean Authorities Urge Banks to Step Up Controls as Won Slumps
Chip rout puts Korea's 'ant' investors to the test as margin debt soars
KOSPI plunges 8%: why Goldman Sachs still sees 12,000
One quadrillion is the number used to describe the wealth effect of South Korea's stock-market miracle
Samsung down 5%, SK Hynix slips 7%: is South Korea too dependent on AI boom?

holdings.infoBox: FLKR is an equity ETF with a total of 162 individual holdings. The top holdings are SK hynix stock at 32.69%, Samsung Electronics at 19.87%, SK Square at 3.85%, Samsung Electro-Mechanics at 3.01%, and Hyundai Motor Company at 2.24%.

overview.description: The Franklin FTSE South Korea ETF (FLKR) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE South Korea RIC Capped index. The fund tracks a market cap-weighted index of large- and mid-cap South Korean companies. FLKR was launched on Nov 2, 2017 and is issued by Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Bank of Korea Warns Inflation May Stay Elevated Despite Middle East Truce
data.text: South Korea is likely to see elevated inflation for a prolonged period as higher energy costs spread into the broader economy and creates fresh risks for policymakers, the Bank of Korea said.

data.text: Gov. Shin Hyun-song 's remarks will likely reinforce market expectations that the central bank will resume tightening as soon as next month.

data.text: South Korea's central bank held rates steady at its first meeting under Gov. Shin Hyun-song, but kept the door open to policy tightening as it forecast higher growth and inflation.
